Motion For Determinating Or/Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf
Off Calendar Nov-17-2005 Continued To Dec-07-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Peter J. Keith for defendant and moving party City and County of San Francisco

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
Defendant's motion for terminating or monetary sanctions is granted in part. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff is ordered to comply with the previous order by November 30, 2005. Failure to obey the previous order and to serve responses was a misuse of the discovery process. Additional monetary sanctions in the amount of $240 are to be paid on or before November 30, 2005. The motion for terminating sanctions is continued to December 7, 2005 at 9:00 a.m. Counsel for defendant shall advise the Court if the plaintiff has complied with the order. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From & To Impose Sanctions On Pltf
Off Calendar Sep-16-2005 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: PETER J. KEITH, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

RULING:
TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel responses to form and special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to provide complete verified responses without objection by October 7, 2005. Failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $240 are to be paid on or before October 7, 2005. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
